rag_rules: Repair only unresolved references and type mismatches in implementation-level AGREE clauses using existing AADL features or locally declared helpers.
generic_rules: Fix the root unresolved identifier before addressing type errors in expressions.
Use only int, real, or bool as scalar types in AGREE declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Assign statements in implementation annexes must target existing output features or auxiliary variables.
Equations and guarantees must use expressions with resolved, compatible types.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types not declared in the model.
Do not add architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors—fix references or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope notes for component type contracts
AGREE assumption and guarantee placement in type scopes
Implementation-level binding syntax for non-validator contexts
